festivals.name: Meenakshi Thirukalyanam (Chithirai Festival)
festivals.description: The celestial wedding of Goddess Meenakshi and Lord Sundareswarar, the emotional and spiritual highlight of the two-week Chithirai Festival, performed with Vedic rituals and drawing over a million devotees to the Meenakshi Amman Temple.

day_wise_highlights.event: Vaasthu Shanti - purification ritual to cleanse the temple grounds before the festival begins
Kodiyetram - flag hoisting marks the official start; evening processions include Karpaga Vriksha and Simha Vahanam
Bootha Vahanam and Anna Vahanam processions, representing protection and nourishment
Kailasa Parvadham and Kamadhenu Vahanam, symbolizing spiritual pursuit and prosperity
Thanga Pallakku - a golden palanquin procession known for its calm, intimate darshan
Thanga Guthirai Vahanam (golden horse) and Vedar Pari Leelai, a mythological enactment
Saiva Samaya Varalaaru recitation and Rishaba Vahanam (sacred bull procession)
Rare darshan of Sri Gangalanathar and the night's Yaali Vahanam procession
Meenakshi Pattabhishekam - the coronation of Goddess Meenakshi as ruler of Madurai
Meenakshi Digvijayam - procession symbolizing her triumphant journey across all directions
Meenakshi Thirukalyanam - the celestial wedding of Meenakshi and Sundareswarar, the spiritual peak of the festival
Therottam - the grand chariot procession pulled through the streets by devotees
Theerthavari (closing water rituals for the Meenakshi festivities) and Edhir Sevai at Thallakulam, welcoming Kallazhagar as he nears the city
Vaigai Aatril Ezhuntharulal - Kallazhagar's symbolic entry into the Vaigai River, arriving after missing the wedding
Sesha Vahanam, Garuda Vahanam, and Dasavatharam Kaatchi depicting the ten incarnations of Vishnu
Mohini Avatharam and Rajanga Alankaram - Vishnu's Mohini form and a regal deity decoration
Kallazhagar begins his return journey toward Alagar Kovil
Kallazhagar returns to Alagar Kovil, completing his journey
Urchava Saatru Murai - closing rituals mark the formal end of the festival

etiquette.tip: Shoulders and knees must be covered when entering Meenakshi Amman Temple; footwear must be removed before entering the temple premises. Men are often expected to remove shirts in the inner sanctum in some southern temples, so check current rules on arrival.

food.description: A cooling Madurai specialty drink made with milk, almond gum (badam pisin), nannari syrup, and ice cream — sold on nearly every street corner but strongest at the shop where it originated.
food.where_to_try: Madurai Famous Jigarthanda, near Vilakkuthoon, Town Hall Road (original branch since 1977)

food.description: Soft, fluffy idlis served with a spiced podi and ghee topping, plated on a banana leaf with four types of chutney and sambar — a Madurai breakfast staple
food.where_to_try: Murugan Idli Shop, West Masi Street (original outlet,since 1960s)

verified_spots.name: Meenakshi Amman Temple
verified_spots.notes: The historic heart of Madurai and center of the Chithirai Festival; among the 275 Paadal Petra Sthalam sacred to Tamil Shaiva tradition. Entry is free; dress code and footwear rules apply.
